expectations.text: Color is applied via `<<#HexColor>>` AFTER the closing semicolon (e.g., `:Send receipt; <<#D5F5E3>>`) — not the deprecated pre-colon form `#D5F5E3:text;`
expectations.evidence: uses DEPRECATED #COLOR:text; pre-colon form, e.g. '#D5F5E3:Charge card;' — not <<#hex>> post-semicolon

expectations.text: At least one step uses `<<#D5F5E3>>` for the success color
At least one step uses `<<#FFCCCC>>` for the error color
Contains an if/endif (or switch/endswitch) for the success/failure branch
start and stop (or end) are both present
Action text labels distinguish success vs error (e.g., 'Send receipt' vs 'Log error') — color is layered on top of textual signal, not the only carrier of meaning
expectations.evidence: text labels distinguish Send receipt / Log error
